reasoning: A 10x increase over the baseline (from an average of ~10 riots per month to >111 riots in a 30-day period) requires a staggering escalation of violence. ACLED defines "Riots" strictly restrictively: the demonstrators themselves must engage in violence or destruction (peaceful protesters attacked by police do not count, nor do non-violent road blockades). To reach 112 riots, Italy would need nearly 4 separate violent mob events every single day for 30 consecutive days. While Italy passed a controversial security bill in Spring 2025 leading to tens of thousands marching in Rome, the demonstrations remain overwhelmingly localized and mostly peaceful with isolated clashes. Achieving 112 officially classified ACLED riots is characteristic of immense, near-civil-war nationwide uprisings (like the peak of the 2023 French riots or 2020 US George Floyd protests), rather than current conditions in Italy. Because achieving this 1000% jump requires an extraordinary outlier event, the probability is set to the lowest permissible floor, while slightly increasing over time to respect expanding uncertainty for future months.
